For example: For Hadith (i): It teaches that performing obligatory prayers, fasting in Ramadan, and adhering to what is lawful and forbidden is sufficient for entering paradise, emphasizing the importance of basic Islamic obligations for salvation. Acceptable answers include descriptions such as for Hadith (i): It teaches that Muslims should speak good words, remain silent if not, be generous to neighbours and guests, emphasizing belief in Allah and the Last Day; for Hadith (ii): It teaches that earning through one's own work is virtuous and preferable; for Hadith (iii): It teaches the unity of the Muslim community, where harm to one affects all; for Hadith (iv): It teaches that helping widows and the poor is equivalent to great acts of worship like jihad, prayer, or fasting. (iv) He who studies the Qur’an is like the owner of tethered camels. If he attends to them he will keep hold of them, but if he lets them loose they will go away.
